title: llm-burnwatch detect --json output
description: The shape of `llm-burnwatch detect --json`'s one-shot output (not `detect --follow`, which streams a distinct, separately-documented newline-delimited format instead). `alert_schema_version` is bumped whenever this shape changes in a way existing consumers should know about -- see `detectors.protocol.ALERT_SCHEMA_VERSION`, the single source of truth this file's `alert_schema_version` const mirrors. When the input log has zero records, `detect --json` prints a minimal object with only `alert_schema_version`/`call_count`/`anomaly_count`/`anomalies` -- every other property below is therefore optional at the schema level, even though a non-empty log always produces all of them together.

alert_schema_version.description: Version of this output shape. Bumped on breaking changes only; purely additive keys do not bump it.

threshold.description: Modified z-score threshold used by the baseline detector for this run, after resolving --threshold/--sensitivity.

sensitivity.description: The --sensitivity preset used for this run (default: "normal"). Scales the baseline/frequency/cusum detectors' thresholds together; stays "normal" when the advanced --threshold escape hatch is used instead.

anomaly_count.description: Number of calls flagged by the baseline z-score detector.

insufficient_data_count.description: Number of calls skipped because their (label, model) group didn't yet have enough history for a baseline comparison.

index.description: Index of the record in the input log.

rule_violation_count.description: Number of hard-limit violations from RulesDetector (--allowed-models/--max-call-cost/--max-trace-cost).

available.description: Whether this log has enough calendar span (>= MIN_SEASONAL_SPAN_DAYS) for a day-of-week x hour-of-day comparison.

frequency_detector_enabled.description: Whether FrequencyDetector actually ran for this call, after applying --frequency-detector auto/on/off.

cusum_detector_enabled.description: Whether CusumDetector actually ran for this call, after applying --cusum-detector on/off.

budget_detector_enabled.description: Whether BudgetDetector actually ran for this call, i.e. whether `llm-burnwatch budget set` has been run.

ml.description: ML cross-check summary, or null if no trained model exists yet at --model-dir.

reason.description: Present when available=false: why the ML cross-check couldn't run (e.g. scikit-learn missing, tampered/corrupted model registry).
